title: Let us celebrate Woman Suffrage Day
sub_topics: 1907 American grant of suffrage to male citizens only
April 30, 1937 plebiscite (447,725 women voted for suffrage)
Code of Professional Responsibility and Accountability
Marcos administration condemnation of campaign sexism
Parish Pastoral Council for Responsible Voting
female SC justices (Muñoz-Palma, Melencio-Herrera, Griño-Aquino, Singh)
International Association of Women Judges (Cape Town)

stances.claim: Hard-won women's suffrage is under assault — not by law but by 'the disturbing conduct of some individuals who treat women as mere props or punchlines in political campaigns'; the 447,725 brave 1937 suffragists 'must be turning in their graves.'

The lawyer-candidate's 'blabbering' that single still-menstruating mothers could sleep with him once a year violates the Code of Professional Responsibility and Accountability, which 'prohibits lawyers from using offensive or improper language' — he risks Comelec disqualification and SC disbarment.
stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal-disciplinary critique of specific candidate's conduct

stances.claim: President Marcos Jr. correctly condemned campaign sexism as 'unacceptable' — a timely reminder from a leader whose father proclaimed April 30 as Woman Suffrage Day.
stances.rhetorical_move: intergenerational-irony framing (son honoring father's pro-women decree)

stances.claim: Filipino women have broken glass ceilings across institutions — presidency (Aquino, Arroyo), Senate (Pecson, Kalaw, Legarda), Supreme Court (Muñoz-Palma, Melencio-Herrera, Griño-Aquino, Singh as president of IAWJ), private business (Tessie Sy Coson and others), and law practice (extensive alphabetical roster).

institutions: Parish Pastoral Council for Responsible Voting
laws_treaties: Code of Professional Responsibility and Accountability
events: Maria Filomena Singh's election to IAWJ presidency (Cape Town)

signature_phrases: must be turning in their graves
props or punchlines in political campaigns
Let us not diminish the legacy
register_markers: HISTORICALLY, SINCE THE RIGHT OF SUFFRAGE WAS GRANTED IN 1907
decision_framework_signals: applies historical-suffragists framing as moral standard for evaluating present-day candidate conduct
uses Code-of-Professional-Responsibility-and-Accountability as doctrinal-disciplinary basis for sanctioning sexist lawyer-candidates
frames glass-ceiling-breaking as multi-institutional achievement requiring institutional recognition (named-roster format)
notable_anecdotes: Romulo Macalintal reminding CJ that Marcos Sr. proclaimed April 30 as Woman Suffrage Day via PD 2346 (March 29, 1984)
Henrietta 'Tita' De Villa as founder, first chair, and chair emeritus of the Parish Pastoral Council for Responsible Voting — CJ's longtime friend
